commits.subject: fix(data): add HTTPS proxy support to geocode-all script
commits.body:
Node.js https.get does not respect the https_proxy/HTTPS_PROXY env var
(unlike curl). In proxy-only environments (CI containers, sandboxed
hosts), direct TCP connections fail with DNS errors while curl succeeds
through the proxy tunnel.

Added HTTP CONNECT tunneling: when https_proxy is set, the script
establishes a CONNECT tunnel through the proxy, then makes HTTPS
requests through the tunneled socket. Falls back to direct connections
when no proxy is configured.

commits.subject: feat(data): geocode all stations — no import without country validation
commits.body:
Previously the pipeline split stations into two paths: "accepted" (had
address+city from OSM tags, auto-labeled ZA) and "skipped" (needed
geocoding). This meant ~95% of border-zone stations without addr:country
tags could enter the import as ZA without any validation.

New approach: every station gets geocoded via Nominatim.
- Transform (step 2) now outputs all elements for geocoding, only
  rejecting those explicitly tagged as non-SA
- geocode-all.mjs (step 3) validates country_code for every station
  and merges data: OSM for street-level detail, Nominatim for admin
  hierarchy (city, suburb, province, country)
- Province coverage jumps from ~40% to ~100%
- Expected runtime: ~70 min for ~4,200 stations (resumable via cache)

Merge strategy tested with mock data — OSM street address wins,
Nominatim admin hierarchy wins, OSM postal code wins with Nominatim
fallback.

Tags: data, osm, geocoding, data-integrity
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: data-extraction
Semver: minor

commits.subject: fix(data): add 3-layer country filter with border zone detection
commits.body:
The addr:country tag only covers ~5% of stations near borders (tested
on 110 elements in Eswatini border zone). Untagged stations with valid
address/city were being auto-accepted as ZA — a confirmed data integrity
bug.

Three-layer filter:
1. addr:country tag rejects non-ZA when present
2. Border zone detection routes untagged near-border stations to reverse
   geocoding instead of auto-accepting
3. Nominatim reverse geocode validates country_code === 'za'

Tested: Eswatini border zone — 5 accepted (tagged ZA), 104 routed to
geocode (untagged), 0 SA stations wrongly excluded.

Tags: data, osm, country-filter, testing

commits.subject: fix(data): replace area filter with bbox + post-filter for reliability
commits.body:
The Overpass area filter area["ISO3166-1"="ZA"] was untested and carries
risks: slower polygon queries, higher timeout risk with way/relation
elements, and temporary unavailability if the ZA boundary relation is
being edited.

Switched to the proven bbox approach with a two-layer country filter:
1. Transform rejects stations with addr:country != ZA
2. Reverse geocode validates country_code === 'za' from Nominatim

This is faster, more reliable, and can be switched to area filter later
if bbox leakage proves significant.

script and RPC function:

1. Missing way/relation OSM elements (only fetching nodes)

3. No update path for changed station data on re-runs
4. No deactivation of stations removed from OSM
5. Bounding box leaks neighboring country stations
6. Province abbreviations (GP, WC, etc.) not mapped
7. Batch file format doesn't match RPC expected input
8. Reverse geocode script not yet implemented
9. No validation of geocode results

commits.subject: feat(data): add Overpass API transform script for OSM fuel station import
commits.body:
Adds a Node.js script that transforms raw Overpass API data (OpenStreetMap
fuel stations in South Africa) into the bulk-import JSON format accepted by
the bulk_insert_stations RPC function.

The Overpass API provides 4,215 fuel stations for SA in a single query.
The transform maps OSM tags to our station schema: brand (85%), phone (63%),
amenities, operating hours, loyalty programs, payment methods, and province.

2,733 stations pass validation with 0 errors. The remaining 1,482 lack
address/city data in OSM and would need reverse geocoding to fill in.

Output is written as batched JSON files (1000 per batch) for safe import
via the admin bulk-import UI.

Usage:
  curl -sL --max-time 120 -X POST \
    -d 'data=[out:json][timeout:100];node[amenity=fuel](-35,16,-22,33);out body;' \
    "https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter" -o data/overpass-raw.json
  node scripts/transform-overpass.mjs

commits.subject: fix(core): resolve 8 runtime and quality issues from code review
commits.body:
- Fix station detail coordinate extraction: parse PostGIS GeoJSON
  instead of accessing non-existent .latitude/.longitude properties
  (was sending users to 0,0 for directions)
- Fix fuel type names: query fuel_prices table (has FK for PostgREST
  join) instead of current_prices materialized view (no FK, join
  silently fails, shows "Unknown" for all fuel types)
- Fix Mapbox token init: FuelMap and StationMarker now import from
  lib/mapbox init module instead of @rnmapbox/maps directly, ensuring
  setAccessToken is called before map renders
- Fix map/list toggle: make views mutually exclusive (list was always
  visible regardless of toggle state)
- Add admin auth warning banner: persistent banner in admin layout
  warns that edits may fail without a valid admin session
- Add updated_at trigger: new migration auto-sets stations.updated_at
  on row update, removing fragile client-side timestamp setting
- Remove dead dark mode branching: root layout hardcodes DefaultTheme
  since all components use light-only styles
- Fix inconsistent imports: standardize to relative paths (was mixing
  @/ alias with ../../ relative paths)
